My website went from 78 to 8,018 monthly Google clicks. The lesson was embarrassingly boring

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. My website went from 78 to 8,018 monthly Google clicks. The lesson was embarrassingly boring
   Concrete distribution lesson with numbers and a replicable playbook.

2. I mapped out why SaaS founders keep building things nobody pays for. Here is the pattern.
   Specific validation heuristic with a clear pre-order test instead of generic advice.

3. If you're shipping agent-built code, do you actually know what your agent refuses to do?
   Good framing for teams trusting coding agents with repo mutations.

4. Show HN: Make PDFs look scanned (CLI or in the browser via WASM)
   Real utility, privacy-friendly local workflow, and clear install/usage docs.

5. Effective use-cases for LLMs
   Specific non-hype workflow ideas from someone using LLMs in normal engineering work.
